page: Amazon Web Services g6.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive, GPU-accelerated server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.4 GHz, with 48 physical cores and multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, the instance integrates four NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 89 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 3760 GB NVMe SSD, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU encryption tests placing in the top 10% of evaluated systems. Single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed, with small-block cached read bandwidth scoring in the top 25%, while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50%. The instance demonstrates exceptional capabilities in machine learning workloads, achieving top-tier performance in LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the g6.24xlarge offers a highly consolidated hardware profile, balancing massive parallel compute with dedicated GPU acceleration and fast local NVMe storage. This makes it highly suitable for demanding workloads such as large language model inference, ray tracing, image processing, and high-throughput database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by 96 vCPUs from dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.4 GHz, it features 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and four NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs with 89 GB of total VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 3760 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity features a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation. This hardware configuration is highly optimized for large-scale AI inference, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, four NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 89 GB VRAM, and 3760 GB NVMe SSD storage for intensive AI and graphics workloads.

tagline: A high-density GPU instance featuring four NVIDIA L4 accelerators and dedicated AMD EPYC processors for demanding machine learning inference.

Four NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs with 89 GB total VRAM
384.0 GB DDR4 memory and 3760 GB local NVMe SSD storage
